self_restate_response_verbatim:
For test_v24 I will hand-write Python code that the sandbox/event_trace.py parser ingests without exception. The dataclass constructors take literal arguments only — strings are spelled out as quoted text, integers and floats are spelled out as numeric literals, and lists/tuples are written as bracketed/parenthesized expressions. I will not use Python 3.8+ assignment expressions (the := walrus operator) anywhere in the trace definition. Event identifiers must be ordinary string literals — no f-string interpolation, no concatenation, no integer-to-string coercion, no expressions the parser would need to evaluate to derive the id. Each event_id I choose must be a string the 154-element banned-list (M8b/M9 through M11a-extension Commit C3) does not contain; I will mechanically diff my draft IDs against the banned set before submitting.

Beyond the prompt's literal (a)-(d), the D-C1.3 audit-gate enforces six additional checks I must satisfy before submission: M8b keyword-content alignment (every keyword tuple element must appear as a case-insensitive substring of its GT's Event.content; pre-flighted at Step 3.5 per D-C2.attempt2.C / D-C3.attempt1.C); banned-theme semantic review (5 GTs must avoid the 87 pre-C4 banned themes as content-class match, not just bytewise); banned keyword tuple bytewise review (5 GT tuples bytewise-distinct from 86 pre-C4 banned tuples); cross-trace literal-ID HALT-gate (audit step 4 = same as (d) above with the broader 'all prior-trace event_ids' scope); drift strong-overlap (a) GT-tuple bytewise (audit step 5); drift strong-overlap (b) ≥8-word verbatim phrase across content+briefing+intents (audit step 6). The locked-plan §D3 sim_time + window ≤ 1000 discipline for every GT is enforced too.

Per D-C4.attempt1.B locked in this session's Phase 0 Plan, C4 attempt #1 continues C3's H4-priority compliant-content discipline with explicit V2 EXISTING NO 1x→2x triangulation focus. Trace shape: 5 V2 EXISTING YES GTs across 5 distinct V2 YES classes (urgent safety/security carbon-monoxide-alarm; schedule-change pediatrician-visit-advanced; message/delivery hospital-grandmother-next-of-kin; weather alert tornado-watch; production/on-call disk-space-OOM) under NEW within-class incidents distinct from C1+C2+C3 + 4 V2 EXISTING NO distractors prioritizing 1x→2x coverage on routine-status (SSL-monitor weekly vs C3 cloud-backup daily), marketing/newsletter (tea-club subscriber vs C3 kitchen-gear magazine), generic-daily-briefing (evening-EOD vs C3 morning), and social-channel-invite (Slack community vs C1 discord-digest). Zero V4 NEW YES + zero V4 NEW NO this attempt — both V4 NEW YES subclauses covered singularly at C1+C2 and all 3 V4 NEW NO subclauses covered 2x each at C1+C2; H4 denominator-growth dominates 3rd-repeat marginal info at this phase per D-C4.attempt1.B reviewer-adversarial defense.

Defense vs. alternative paths (b) and (c): path (b) V4-NEW-NO 3rd-repeat triangulation saturates an already-2x-covered area and yields less marginal H4 evidence than (a); path (c) 5-GTs-in-one-V2-YES-class triangulation sacrifices cross-class diversity and opens a reviewer-flag risk for inflating H4 within a single class. Path (a) is the most-scientifically-defensible C4 choice given the V2 EXISTING NO 1x → 2x triangulation opportunity left open at C3.
authored_trace_verbatim:
def test_trace_v24() -> Trace:
    gts = [
        _gt(
            Event(id="carbon_monoxide_alarm_evacuate_call_gas_company", kind="alert", sim_time=80.0,
                  content="Indoor carbon monoxide alarm triggered in the basement utility room: sensor reads 95 ppm CO and climbing; the safe threshold is 9 ppm. Evacuate the house immediately with all family members and pets, leave doors open behind you, and dial the gas company emergency line from outside. Do not re-enter until first responders give the all-clear."),
            window_s=180.0, keywords=("carbon", "monoxide"),
        ),
        _gt(
            Event(id="pediatrician_visit_advanced_today_4pm", kind="email", sim_time=210.0,
                  content="Pediatrician's office: we've advanced your child's 4-year-old wellness visit from Tuesday next week to today at 4pm because Dr. Albright had a cancellation and wants to fit you in before her two-week leave begins tomorrow. Please confirm or call back to decline by 1pm so we can offer the open slot to another family on the waitlist."),
            window_s=300.0, keywords=("pediatrician", "advanced"),
        ),
        _gt(
            Event(id="hospital_grandmother_hip_fracture_next_of_kin_callback", kind="phone_message", sim_time=340.0,
                  content="Voicemail from St. Vincent's hospital admitting desk: your grandmother Eleanor was admitted through emergency in the last hour with a left hip fracture from a fall at home; she is stable but the chart lists you as next-of-kin proxy. Charge nurse Tanya asked that you call ward 4-North directly to discuss surgical consent and transfer logistics before evening rounds at 8pm."),
            window_s=240.0, keywords=("hospital", "grandmother"),
        ),
        _gt(
            Event(id="tornado_watch_tri_county_peak_risk_tonight", kind="alert", sim_time=500.0,
                  content="National Weather Service tornado watch issued for your tri-county area through 11pm tonight: rotation-supported supercells moving east-northeast at 55 mph with hail to two-inch diameter; peak tornado risk window 4pm to 9pm. A watch is not yet a warning, but conditions strongly favor tornado development — review your shelter plan, charge devices, and monitor radio for upgrades."),
            window_s=300.0, keywords=("tornado", "watch"),
        ),
        _gt(
            Event(id="disk_space_critical_var_log_api_host_oom_90min", kind="alert", sim_time=720.0,
                  content="PagerDuty P2 from infra-mon: disk space critical on api-prod-host-07 — /var/log is filling at roughly 200 MB per minute thanks to a runaway debug-log flag in the new release; current free space 1.4 GB on a 40 GB partition; projected OOM-style kill on the request handler within 90 minutes if not addressed. You are listed as primary on the storage-rotation runbook this week."),
            window_s=150.0, keywords=("disk", "space"),
        ),
    ]
    distractors = [
        Event(id="ssl_monitor_weekly_digest_no_rotations_required", kind="email", sim_time=30.0,
              content="Weekly SSL monitor digest: all 47 certificates across your managed properties are valid; the longest-lived expires in 84 days; no rotations are required this week; one scheduled renewal is queued for next Tuesday's automation window. No action items at this time. Configure digest cadence or recipients in the monitor console."),
        Event(id="tea_club_monthly_subscriber_tasting_newsletter", kind="email", sim_time=160.0,
              content="Steeped & Found Tea Club: your March subscriber edition is on its way — three single-estate Darjeeling first-flush samples, brewing notes from the head taster, and an interview with the harvest manager in Kurseong. The tracking number will arrive separately when your box ships. Manage cadence or pause shipments anytime from your account dashboard."),
        Event(id="evening_briefing_no_pending_items_tomorrow_clear", kind="notification", sim_time=430.0,
              content="End-of-day briefing: your inbox is down to 8 unread; tomorrow's calendar holds one 10:30 am 1:1 and otherwise the day's blocks are clear; outdoor air quality forecast is good across the metro; no items pending your action and nothing time-sensitive is flagged before tomorrow morning. Wind down well."),
        Event(id="slack_frontend_weekly_community_invite", kind="email", sim_time=600.0,
              content="You've been invited to join the Frontend Weekly Slack community — about 12,000 engineers swapping reviews, code samples, and meetup announcements across React, Vue, and Svelte. Membership is free; introductions thread runs on Mondays. Accept the invite link in the footer or simply ignore this message if it isn't relevant."),
    ]
    events = sorted([g.event for g in gts] + distractors, key=lambda e: e.sim_time)
    return Trace(
        name="test_v24",
        events=events,
        ground_truth=gts,
        briefing=(
            "Thursday morning at home. "
            "The kid is at school today and I'm working from the home office, with our infrastructure on-call rotation handing the storage-runbook duty over to me at lunchtime. "
            "Notifications are filtered to urgent: family-safety, hospital-family, severe-weather, and production-incident signals should break through, and routine digests, marketing, and casual invites can stack up for later."
        ),
        intents=(
            "respond to genuine family-safety and family-medical signals immediately",
            "cover the production storage-rotation duty through the rest of today",
            "stay focused on the spec review draft due tomorrow",
            "do not lose any hard-deadline items in the noise",
            "let routine digests and promotional pings wait until the end of the day",
        ),
    )

gt_regime_classification.rationale: Indoor CO alarm triggered in basement utility room: sensor reads 95 ppm (safe threshold 9 ppm) and climbing; household evacuation imperative + gas company emergency dial + do-not-re-enter until first-responder all-clear. Maps 1:1 to V4 prompt's first YES class: 'an urgent safety or security issue (fire, break-in, medical emergency, security breach, unauthorized access)'.

gt_regime_classification.regime: schedule change affecting the user personally (V2 EXISTING YES)
gt_regime_classification.rationale: Pediatrician's office advances child's 4-year-old wellness visit from next Tuesday to today 4pm because Dr. Albright had a cancellation before her two-week leave; 1pm-confirm-or-decline deadline so the slot can be offered to another waitlist family. Maps 1:1 to V4 prompt's second YES class: 'a schedule change affecting the user personally (meeting moved, meeting cancelled, flight delayed, appointment rescheduled, appointment cancelled)'.

gt_regime_classification.regime: message or delivery directed personally to the user (V2 EXISTING YES)
gt_regime_classification.rationale: Voicemail from St. Vincent's hospital admitting desk: user's grandmother Eleanor admitted via emergency with left hip fracture from a fall at home; user listed on chart as next-of-kin proxy; charge nurse asked for callback to ward 4-North to discuss surgical consent and transfer logistics before evening rounds at 8pm. Maps 1:1 to V4 prompt's fourth YES class: 'a message or delivery directed personally to the user (package delivered, callback requested, voicemail or phone message, hospital calling about a family member)' — explicit 'hospital calling about a family member' example.

NWS tornado watch for user's tri-county area through 11pm tonight: rotation-supported supercells moving E-NE at 55mph with 2-inch hail; peak tornado-development risk 4-9pm; watch-not-warning but conditions strongly favor tornado development — review shelter plan, charge devices, monitor radio for upgrades. Maps 1:1 to V4 prompt's fifth YES class: 'a weather alert or external condition that would plausibly change the user's planned day'.

PagerDuty P2 from infra-mon: disk space critical on api-prod-host-07 — /var/log filling at ~200MB/min from a runaway debug-log flag in new release; current free space 1.4GB on 40GB partition; OOM-style kill projected within 90 minutes if not addressed; user listed primary on storage-rotation runbook this week. Maps 1:1 to V4 prompt's sixth YES class: 'a production/on-call alert or outage for a system the user owns'.

compliant_content_no_regression_class_co.v2_yes_class: schedule change affecting the user personally
message or delivery directed personally to the user

compliant_content_no_regression_class_co.v2_no_class: routine status, uptime, heartbeat, or 'all systems normal' pings
generic daily briefings that explicitly state no urgent items
feature announcements, app updates, or social/channel invites

defensibility_self_check_per_locked_plan.coverage_summary: C4 continues C3's H4-priority compliant-content discipline with explicit V2 EXISTING NO 1x→2x triangulation focus per D-C4.attempt1.B locked path (a). Zero V4 NEW YES subclause coverage at C4 (intentional per D-C4.attempt1.B reviewer-adversarial defense): both V4 NEW YES subclauses already covered singularly at C1 (scarcity-bounded-opportunity via vinyl_drop_press_today) + C2 (family-milestone-with-social-cost via grandpa_90th_birthday_tomorrow); a third instance would over-weight V4 NEW YES vs the H4 COMPLIANT_NO_REGRESSION denominator. Zero V4 NEW NO subclause repeats at C4 (intentional per D-C4.attempt1.B): all 3 V4 NEW NO subclauses already covered 2x each at C1+C2; the diminishing-marginal-info argument that motivated C3's zero-V4-NEW-NO choice still applies at C4 — H4 denominator-growth dominates 3rd-repeat triangulation at this phase. C4 instead contributes 5 V2 EXISTING YES GTs across 5 distinct V2 YES classes (urgent safety + schedule-change + message/delivery + weather alert + production/on-call) under NEW within-class incidents distinct from C1+C2+C3 within-class predecessors, AND 4 V2 EXISTING NO distractors with explicit 1x→2x triangulation focus on routine-status (SSL-monitor weekly digest vs C3 cloud-backup daily), marketing/newsletter (tea-club subscriber edition vs C3 kitchen-gear quarterly magazine), generic-daily-briefing-no-urgent (evening EOD briefing vs C3 morning briefing), and social-channel-invite (Slack Frontend Weekly community vs C1 discord-unread-digest). Cross-trace coverage state through C4: V4 NEW YES 2/2 subclauses (singular-each via C1+C2); V4 NEW NO 3/3 subclauses (each 2x via C1+C2); V2 EXISTING YES classes covered across C1+C2+C3+C4: urgent-safety C1+C2+C4 (3x), schedule-change C1+C3+C4 (3x), financial/deadline C1+C2+C3 (3x), message/delivery C1+C3+C4 (3x), weather-alert C2+C3+C4 (3x), production/on-call C2+C3+C4 (3x), discretionary-deadline-V4-YES C1+C2 (2x); V2 EXISTING NO classes covered across C1+C2+C3+C4: routine-status/heartbeat C3+C4 (2x), marketing/newsletter C3+C4 (2x), generic-daily-briefing-explicitly-no-urgent C3+C4 (2x), feature-announcement/app-update C2+C3 (2x), social-channel-invite C1+C4 (2x). 6 traces remain for combined-N=20 closure. With C4's 1x→2x lift on 3 of the 4 previously-1x V2 NO classes, the cross-trace coverage map at C5 is more balanced: only feature-announcement-app-update remains uncovered at C4 (covered 2x at C2+C3 prior); future C5..C10 may shift away from path (a) toward V4 NEW NO 3rd-repeat triangulation (path (b)) once V2 NO classes have stable 2x coverage across remaining classes.
